conflict: Routing prefers locality (keep related work physically close) but memory prefers recency (recently touched items are most relevant) - under drift the two recommend opposite placements.
candidates: recency is a proxy for relevance, not its cause; under drift the proxy detaches from locality
locality and recency optimise different costs (retrieval latency vs. staleness) and only conflict when drift is fast
context: Surfaced as an open Layer-9 conflict between two active claims about placement policy.

conflict: Privacy requires forgetting raw inputs, but provenance requires keeping enough of them to audit a claim's origin.
